channel.title: Home on FsFlow
channel.description: Recent content in Home on FsFlow

item.title: FsToolkit.ErrorHandling
item.description:
FsToolkit.ErrorHandling

If you are coming from FsToolkit.ErrorHandling, you will find that FsFlow is orthogonal rather than a direct replacement. While both libraries help with result-based programming, FsFlow focuses on a unified execution model that carries environments and runtime policies.

The Model Difference

FsToolkit.ErrorHandling provides a broad toolbox of helpers for working with Result, AsyncResult, and TaskResult as separate, wrapped types.

FsFlow provides a single, scalable progression:

[Check](/reference/check/) -> [Result](/reference/result/) -> [Validation](/reference/validation/) -> [Flow](/reference/flow/)

In FsFlow, the environment and runtime concerns are baked into the computation, allowing you to write orchestration logic that remains agnostic of whether the underlying work is sync or async until it hits the application boundary.

item.title: Getting Started
item.description:
Getting Started

FsFlow is a toolkit for building robust, Result-based programs in F#. It allows you to scale from simple validation logic to complex, effectful application boundaries using a single, unified mental model.

1. The Continuum of Logic

FsFlow is designed around a continuum. You should always use the simplest tool that satisfies your current requirement:

Pure Checks -> Result & Validation -> Flow
  • Pure Checks: Reusable predicates for basic validation.
  • Result & Validation: Domain logic that handles success or failure (either fail-fast or error-accumulating).
  • Flow: The application boundary where you need dependencies, async/task interop, logging, or cancellation.

2. Start with Checks and Results

Most logic starts pure. Use Check for reusable predicates and Result for domain logic.

item.title: Pure Checks
item.description:
Pure Checks

Check<'value> is the foundation of FsFlow validation. It is a reusable predicate layer that returns Result<'value, unit>.

  • If the predicate is true, it returns Ok value.
  • If the predicate is false, it returns Error ().

By returning unit on failure, Check stays agnostic about your application’s error types. You decide what the error means at the boundary where you use it.

The Two Shapes of Check

  1. Value-Preserving: Check<'value> - returns the input value on success. 
    let check = Check.notBlank // string -> Result<string, unit>
  2. Gate: Check<unit> - returns unit on success (useful for yes/no questions). 
    let check = Check.okIf (age > 18) // unit -> Result<unit, unit>

Attaching Errors with Check.orError

Use Check.orError to turn a unit failure into a meaningful application error.

item.title: Ref (Atomic References)
item.description:
Ref (Atomic References)

Ref<'T> is a handle to a mutable reference that can be updated atomically. It is the simplest way to manage shared state between multiple concurrent parts of your application.

Note: Ref is currently available on .NET only.

Why Use Ref?

In a functional program, state is usually passed as parameters or through the environment. However, some scenarios—like a shared counter, a cache, or a status flag—require multiple concurrent workflows to observe and update the same value.

item.title: Environment Slicing
item.description:
Environment Slicing (The Record Pattern)

The Record Pattern is the most common way to manage dependencies in FsFlow. It uses standard F# records to define the “world” a workflow lives in.

Projecting from the Environment

When a workflow needs a dependency, it “reads” it from the environment record.

type AppEnv = 
    { Gateway: IPingGateway
      Logger: ILogger }

let ping =
    flow {
        // 'env' is the full AppEnv record
        let! gateway = Flow.read (fun env -> env.Gateway)
        return! gateway.Ping()
    }

The Shorthand: _.Field

F# provides a nice shorthand for these simple projections. Instead of (fun env -> env.Gateway), you can write _.Gateway. This makes the code much cleaner and easier to read.

item.title: Execution Semantics
item.description:
Execution Semantics

FsFlow uses a unified Flow<'env, 'error, 'value> model that handles synchronous code, F# Async, and .NET Task interop natively.

Success and Typed Failure

Every Flow execution results in an Exit<'value, 'error>:

  • Exit.Success value: The happy path.
  • Exit.Failure (Cause.Fail error): An expected domain-specific failure.
  • Exit.Failure Cause.Interrupt: The workflow was signaled to stop (e.g., cancellation).
  • Exit.Failure (Cause.Die exception): An unexpected defect or crash.

All standard combinators (map, bind, zip, orElse) are short-circuiting. The first Exit.Failure stops the workflow unless explicitly caught.

item.title: Result CE
item.description:
Result CE

Use the result {} computation expression when you have a sequence of steps where each step depends on the previous one, and you want to stop at the first failure.

This is “fail-fast” semantics.

Basic Usage

The result {} builder binds standard F# Result<'value, 'error> types.

type UserError = | MissingName | MissingEmail

let validateUser name email =
    result {
        // If name is blank, it returns Error MissingName and stops.
        let! validName = name |> Check.notBlank |> Check.orError MissingName
        
        // This line only runs if the name was valid.
        let! validEmail = email |> Check.notBlank |> Check.orError MissingEmail
        
        return { Name = validName; Email = validEmail }
    }

Guarded Bindings

Guard is a powerful companion to result {}. It allows you to bind boolean or check-shaped sources directly while attaching an error value at the binding site.

item.title: Schedule (Retry & Repeat)
item.description:
Schedule (Retry & Repeat)

The Schedule module provides a powerful DSL for describing policies that govern how and when a workflow should be executed again. You can use schedules to implement sophisticated retry strategies (like exponential backoff with jitter) or recurring tasks.

Note: Schedule is currently available on .NET only.

Basic Schedules

A schedule decides two things:

  1. Whether to continue (recur).
  2. How long to wait before the next attempt.

Fixed Number of Recursions

// Recur 5 times (6 attempts total)
let fiveTimes = Schedule.recurs 5

Fixed Spacing

// Recur indefinitely with 1 second between attempts
let everySecond = Schedule.spaced (TimeSpan.FromSeconds 1.0)

Exponential Backoff

// Delays: 100ms, 200ms, 400ms, 800ms...
let backoff = Schedule.exponential (TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds 100.0)

Adding Jitter

Jitter adds randomness to delays to prevent “thundering herd” problems in distributed systems. Schedule.jittered adds a random factor between 0.5x and 1.5x to the current delay.

item.title: Troubleshooting Types
item.description:
Troubleshooting Types

This page shows the compiler errors that usually mean you crossed a wrapper boundary in the wrong place.

Most FsFlow type errors are not exotic. The compiler usually sees one wrapper shape and you intended another.

Error: A Unique Overload For Method Bind Could Not Be Determined

This usually happens when the compiler cannot tell which wrapper shape a let! value should use.

Example:

let nested : Async<Async<Result<int, string>>> =
    async {
        return async { return Ok 42 }
    }

let workflow : Flow<unit, string, int> =
    flow {
        let! next = nested
        let! value = next
        return value
    }

The second let! is ambiguous.

item.title: Validus Integration
item.description:
Validus Integration

This page shows how FsFlow can fit beside Validus validation pipelines.

Validus is a strong choice when the problem is still validation, especially when you want a richer DSL, composition, accumulation, or value-object style checks.

FsFlow can usually begin after that work is done.

Validus and FsFlow.Check fit especially well together: Validus can handle richer validation rules, while FsFlow.Check stays available for smaller pure guards that feed directly into Result, Validation, or Flow.

item.title: The Guard
item.description:
The Guard

The Guard is a bridge. It allows pure predicate checks and simple error-bearing sources (like Option or Result) to fail a computation with a specific domain error.

While builders like flow {} and result {} can bind many types directly, Guard is the primary tool for assigning a domain-specific error to a source or “unwrapping” an effectful source before validation.

Why do I need Guard?

Consider a simple user lookup. When it is a pure function, it works seamlessly with Check:

item.title: Benchmarks
item.description:
Benchmarks

This page shows the performance tradeoffs of using FsFlow compared to manual result/async/task composition.

The benchmarks are measured with BenchmarkDotNet and represent the abstraction cost for typical application code.

Summary

  • abstraction cost is negligible for effectful code
  • cold execution for task-oriented computations adds small allocation overhead compared to hot ValueTask
  • performance is competitive with manual Async<Result<'T, 'E>> composition

Setup

All measurements were taken on:

  • .NET 8.0
  • Ubuntu 22.04
  • AMD Ryzen 9 5950X

Abstraction Cost

Measures a 20-step bind sequence where each step is pure. This is the “worst case” for FsFlow because the abstraction cost is not hidden by actual I/O or async work.

item.title: Capabilities (CAPS)
item.description:
Capabilities (CAPS)

The CAPS pattern lets you decouple your workflows from specific record types. Instead of saying “I need the AppEnv record,” a workflow says “I need any environment that provides an IClock.”

This is achieved using the Needs<'dep> interface and the Env<'dep> request token.

Requesting a Dependency with Env<'dep>

In a CAPS workflow, you use the Env<'dep> token to request a dependency by its type. FsFlow handles finding that dependency in the environment for you.

item.title: IcedTasks Integration
item.description:
IcedTasks Integration

This page shows how FsFlow can fit beside IcedTasks.

IcedTasks is a natural neighbor when the codebase already uses task computation expressions and cold or cancellable task helpers.

FsFlow does not need to replace that layer. It can add typed failure, environment threading, and boundary management where those concerns matter.

IcedTasks is especially interesting when you want task-native ergonomics and performance-sensitive helpers. FsFlow stays in the picture when you want to carry a typed result and an explicit environment together with the runtime shape.

item.title: STM (Software Transactional Memory)
item.description:
STM (Software Transactional Memory)

Software Transactional Memory (STM) is a powerful concurrency primitive that allows you to combine multiple atomic operations into a single transaction.

While Ref is perfect for updating a single variable, STM is designed for scenarios where you need to update multiple variables consistently. FsFlow ensures that the entire transaction is executed atomically—either all changes are committed, or none are.

Note: STM is currently available on .NET only.

item.title: Straightforward Examples
item.description:
Straightforward Examples

These examples show how to use FsFlow for common tasks without the overhead of a full application setup.

1. Simple Environment Access

Use Flow.read to project a single field from your environment record.

type Config = { ApiUrl: string }

let getUrl =
    flow {
        let! url = Flow.read _.ApiUrl
        return url
    }

let outcome = getUrl |> Flow.run { ApiUrl = "https://api.example.com" }
// outcome = Exit.Success "https://api.example.com"

2. Combining Pure Logic and Async Work

Use flow {} to mix pure Check logic, Async blocks, and other flows.

let validateId id =
    id |> okIf (id > 0) |> orError "Invalid ID"

let fetchUser id =
    async { return { Id = id; Name = "Ada" } }

let workflow id =
    flow {
        let! validId = validateId id
        let! user = fetchUser validId // Binds Async<'T> directly
        return user.Name
    }

let effect = workflow 42 |> Flow.run ()
// effect = Effect<string, string> (ValueTask or Async)

3. Retrying a Flow

Use the Schedule module to add operational policies like retries.

item.title: Task and Async Interop
item.description:
Task and Async Interop

FsFlow provides a single, unified computation expression—flow {}—that handles synchronous code, F# Async, and .NET Task interop natively. You don’t have to choose between different builders; the same flow can orchestrate all these effect types.

Direct Binds

Inside a flow {} block, you can use let! to bind many common F# and .NET types directly. FsFlow handles the conversion to its internal execution model automatically.

Type Outcome
Flow<'env, 'error, 'value> Continues with the flow’s value.
Result<'value, 'error> Continues on Ok, short-circuits on Error.
Async<'value> Awaits the async and continues with the value.
Async<Result<'value, 'error>> Awaits the async and handles the Result outcome.
Task<'value> Awaits the task and continues with the value.
Task<Result<'value, 'error>> Awaits the task and handles the Result outcome.
ValueTask<'value> Awaits the value task and continues with the value.
ValueTask<Result<'value, 'error>> Awaits and handles the Result outcome.

Example: Mixed Orchestration

let fetchUser (id: int) : Task<User> = ...
let validate (user: User) : Result<User, string> = ...
let saveUser (user: User) : Async<unit> = ...

let processUser id =
    flow {
        // Bind a .NET Task
        let! user = fetchUser id
        
        // Bind a Result
        let! validUser = validate user
        
        // Bind an F# Async
        do! saveUser validUser
        
        return "Done"
    }

Option and ValueOption

Option<'value> and ValueOption<'value> can also be bound directly, but only if the flow’s error type is unit.

item.title: Validate CE
item.description:
Validate CE

Use the validate {} computation expression when you have multiple independent checks and you want to collect every failure into a single report.

While the standard result {} or flow {} blocks “fail fast” (stopping at the first error), validate {} continues checking even after a failure occurs. This is often called “accumulating” semantics.

Accumulating with and!

The key to accumulation is the and! keyword. Steps joined by and! are evaluated independently, and their errors are merged into a Diagnostics graph.

item.title: Architectural Styles
item.description:
Architectural Styles

This page shows how the FsFlow workflow family should fit into your application architecture without forcing one app shape on every codebase.

FsFlow supports three valid architectural styles.

Three related workflow families. Three valid architectural styles. Choose based on app shape and team preferences.

1. Booted App Environment

Use this when your application already has a conventional booted runtime and passing one explicit application environment keeps composition simple.

item.title: Diagnostics Graph
item.description:
Diagnostics Graph

Unlike many validation libraries that return a flat list of error strings, FsFlow returns a structured Diagnostics Graph. This allows you to represent exactly where in a complex data structure a failure occurred.

The Structure

The graph is a tree where each node can contain local errors and child branches.

type PathSegment =
    | Key of string    // A record field or property
    | Index of int     // A position in a list or sequence
    | Name of string   // A custom label for a validation branch

type Diagnostic<'error> =
    { Path: PathSegment list
      Error: 'error }

type Diagnostics<'error> =
    { Errors: 'error list
      Children: Map<PathSegment, Diagnostics<'error>> }

Creating Scoped Diagnostics

Inside a validate {} block, you use helpers to push diagnostics into the tree:

item.title: FSharpPlus Integration
item.description:
FSharpPlus Integration

This page shows how FsFlow can fit beside codebases that already use FSharpPlus.

FSharpPlus is a broad functional base library. That makes it different from the more targeted integration stories like FsToolkit.ErrorHandling, Validus, and IcedTasks.

FsFlow can sit on top of that style where orchestration begins.

FSharpPlus is strongest when the codebase already relies on broad generic operations and monad transformer stacks. That strength comes with a cost: more compiler work, more abstraction to keep in your head, and error surfaces that can be harder to read when you are trying to understand a FsFlow boundary.

item.title: Stream (FlowStream)
item.description:
Stream (FlowStream)

FlowStream<'env, 'error, 'value> represents a cold, pull-based asynchronous stream. Like Flow, a FlowStream requires an environment to run and can fail with a typed error. It is built on top of .NET’s IAsyncEnumerable, providing native support for backpressure and asynchronous iteration.

Note: FlowStream is currently available on .NET only.

Creating a Stream

From a Sequence

The simplest way to create a stream is from an existing seq.

let numbers = FlowStream.fromSeq [ 1 .. 10 ]

Transforming Streams

Mapping Values

You can transform the successful values in a stream using FlowStream.map.

item.title: Effect-TS Comparison
item.description:
Effect-TS Comparison

This page shows FsFlow in relation to Effect-TS without pretending they solve the same problem at the same scale.

What Carries Over

These ideas are shared:

  • typed success and error channels
  • explicit dependency access
  • compositional workflow values
  • runtime helpers for retry, timeout, and cancellation-aware execution

What Is Different

FsFlow is aimed at ordinary F# application code:

  • flow {} instead of generator-based syntax, with direct binding for Async, Task, ValueTask, and ColdTask
  • first-class interop with Result, Async, and .NET Task
  • explicit environment reads such as Flow.read _.Gateway or Flow.read _.Gateway
  • a much smaller surface focused on application flows rather than a broader runtime platform

What Effect-TS Still Has That FsFlow Does Not

Effect-TS is much broader and more mature. FsFlow does not try to match:

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.all
item.description:
Returns success when every check in the sequence succeeds.

Remarks

Sequentially evaluates each check in the checks sequence. Stops at the first failure.

Parameters

  • checks: A sequence of checks.

Returns

A Check that succeeds only if all inputs succeed.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.and
item.description:
Returns success when both checks succeed.

Remarks

This is a logical “and” operation. It short-circuits: if left fails, right is not evaluated.

Parameters

  • left: The first check.
  • right: The second check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds only if both inputs succeed.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.any
item.description:
Returns success when at least one check in the sequence succeeds.

Remarks

Sequentially evaluates each check in the checks sequence. Stops at the first success.

Parameters

  • checks: A sequence of checks.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if any input succeeds.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.apply
item.description: Applies a flow-wrapped function to a flow-wrapped value.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.apply
item.description:
Applies a validation-wrapped function to a validation-wrapped value.

Parameters

  • validation: The validation containing the function.
  • value: The validation containing the value.

Returns

The result of applying the function to the value, with accumulated errors.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.at
item.description:
Scopes a validation under the supplied path segments.

Parameters

  • path: The path segments to apply to the validation.
  • validation: The validation to scope.

Returns

A validation nested under the given path.

item.title: FsFlow.STM.atomically
item.description:
Executes an STM transaction atomically within a flow.

Parameters

  • transaction: The STM transaction to execute.

Returns

A flow that performs the transaction and returns its result.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.bind
item.description:
Sequences a synchronous continuation after a successful value.

Remarks

This is the “flatmap” operation for Flow. It allows for dependent steps where the second flow depends on the value produced by the first.

Parameters

  • binder: A function that takes the successful value and returns a new flow.
  • flow: The source flow to sequence.

Returns

A Flow representing the combined workflow.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.bind
item.description:
Sequences a validation-producing continuation.

Remarks

This is the monadic “bind” for validation. Note that this operation short-circuits and does not accumulate errors from the binder if the source has already failed. For accumulation, use map2 or the applicative and! syntax.

Parameters

  • binder: A function of type 'value -> Validation<'next, 'error>.
  • validation: The source validation.

Returns

The result of the binder or the original diagnostics.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.blank
item.description:
Returns success when the string is blank.

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the string is blank.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.catch
item.description:
Catches exceptions raised during execution and maps them to a typed error.

Remarks

Exceptions that are not caught by this helper will bubble up to the caller of run. This ensures that known exceptions can be handled within the flow context.

Parameters

  • handler: A function of type exn -> 'error to map the exception.
  • flow: The source flow of type Flow to monitor.

Returns

A Flow that converts exceptions into success-path errors.

item.title: FsFlow.Check
item.description:
A reusable predicate result that either preserves a value on success or acts as a gate with unit on success, while carrying a unit failure placeholder until the caller maps it into a domain-specific error.

Remarks

Use the Check module helpers to create and compose checks.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.collect
item.description:
Collects a sequence of validations into a single validation of a list.

Remarks

This operation is applicative: it will collect errors from ALL items in the sequence.

Parameters

  • validations: A sequence of type seq<Validation<'value, 'error>>.

Returns

A validation containing the list of values or accumulated diagnostics.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.delay
item.description: Defers flow construction until execution time.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.EnvironmentVariableErrors.describe
item.description: Formats a human-readable description for an error.

item.title: FsFlow.Diagnostic
item.description: A single failure item attached to a path in a validation graph.

item.title: FsFlow.Diagnostics
item.description:
A mergeable validation graph that carries local errors and nested child branches.

Remarks

`Errors` holds the application errors attached exactly to the current node, while `Children` holds nested branches keyed by `PathSegment`. This structure allows hierarchical validation to stay navigable before flattening. Use `flatten` to convert it into a linear list.

item.title: FsFlow.DiagnosticsModule.empty
item.description:
Creates an empty diagnostics graph with no errors.

Returns

An empty Diagnostics.

item.title: FsFlow.Env
item.description:
Request token for projecting a value from a dependency.

Remarks

Builders read the dependency from the environment, apply the projection, and then reuse the existing lift/bind behavior for the projected value. If the projection returns a Result, Async, Task, ValueTask, ColdTask, option, or voption, the existing workflow rules still apply.

Examples

type IClock =
     abstract UtcNow : unit -> DateTimeOffset

 type ClockCaps =
     inherit Needs<IClock>
     abstract Clock : IClock

 let readClockNow : Flow<#ClockCaps, unit, DateTimeOffset> =
     flow {
         let! now = Env<IClock> _.UtcNow
         return now
     }

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.env
item.description:
Reads the current environment as the flow value.

Remarks

Use this when the entire environment object is needed for the next step of the workflow. For projecting specific properties, read is generally more ergonomic.

Returns

A Flow whose successful value is the current environment.

item.title: FsFlow.CapabilityModule.environment
item.description: Reads the application environment from the environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.EnvironmentVariableError
item.description: Describes a meaningful environment-variable failure.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.equal
item.description:
Returns success when the values are equal.

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values are equal.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.error
item.description: Creates a failing synchronous flow.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.error
item.description:
Creates a failing validation result with the provided diagnostics.

Parameters

  • diagnostics: The Diagnostics graph.

Returns

A failing Validation.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Process.ProcessModule.execute
item.description: Executes a process using the process environment and returns the result.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.FileSystem.FileSystemModule.exists
item.description: Checks if a file exists using the file system environment.

item.title: FsFlow.ScheduleModule.exponential
item.description: Creates a schedule that recurs with exponential backoff.

item.title: FsFlow.ScheduleModule.exponential
item.description: Creates a schedule that recurs with exponential backoff.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.fail
item.description:
Alias for error that reads well in some call sites.

Examples

let flow = Flow.fail "error"
 let result = Flow.run () flow
 // result = Failure (Cause.Fail "error")

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.fail
item.description:
Alias for error.

Parameters

  • diagnostics: The Diagnostics graph.

Returns

A failing Validation.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIf
item.description:
Returns success when the condition is false.

Parameters

  • cond: The boolean condition to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if cond is false.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfBlank
item.description:
Returns the string when it is blank.

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check containing the blank string.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfEmpty
item.description:
Returns the sequence when it is not empty.

Parameters

  • coll: The sequence to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-empty sequence.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns the string when it is null or empty.

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check containing the empty or null string.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are equal.

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values are equal.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfNone
item.description:
Returns the value when the option is Some.

Parameters

  • opt: The option to check.

Returns

A Check containing the value if present.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfNonEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns success when the string is null or empty.

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the string is null or empty.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfNotBlank
item.description:
Returns success when the string is blank.

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the string is blank.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfNotEmpty
item.description:
Returns success when the sequence is empty.

Parameters

  • coll: The sequence to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the sequence is empty.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfNotEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are not equal.

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values differ.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfNotNull
item.description:
Returns success when the value is null.

Parameters

  • value: The value to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the value is null.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfNull
item.description:
Returns the value when it is null.

Parameters

  • value: The value to check.

Returns

A Check containing the null value.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfSome
item.description:
Returns success when the option is None.

Parameters

  • opt: The option to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the option is None.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfValueNone
item.description:
Returns the value when the value option is ValueSome.

Parameters

  • opt: The value option to check.

Returns

A Check containing the value if present.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.failIfValueSome
item.description:
Returns success when the value option is ValueNone.

Parameters

  • opt: The value option to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the value option is ValueNone.

item.title: FsFlow.Fiber
item.description: Represents a handle to a running workflow.

item.title: FsFlow.DiagnosticsModule.flatten
item.description:
Flattens the structured diagnostics graph into a linear list of diagnostics.

Remarks

During flattening, child paths are accumulated from the root down into each emitted diagnostic. The tree itself stores only local errors and child branches, while Diagnostic is reserved for reporting output.

Parameters

  • graph: The Diagnostics to flatten.

Returns

A list of type Diagnostic list.

item.title: FsFlow.Builders.flow
item.description:
The universal flow { } computation expression.

Remarks

Use this builder when the boundary can mix synchronous values, `Async`, `Task`, `Result`, and environment requests while keeping typed failures and explicit dependency access. It preserves the current environment model while allowing the workflow to compose task-oriented inputs directly, so callers do not need to switch builders just to cross an async boundary.

Examples

 let greeting =
     flow {
         let! name = Flow.env
         let! suffix = async { return "!" }
         return $"Hello, {name}{suffix}"
     }

item.title: FsFlow.Flow
item.description: Represents a cold workflow that reads an environment, returns a typed result, and is executed explicitly through Flow.run.

item.title: flow { }
item.description:
The flow { } builder is the primary entry point for orchestrating synchronous, async, and task-based work.

Builder

  • FsFlow.Builders.flow: The universal flow { } computation expression.

item.title: FsFlow.FlowStream
item.description: Represents a cold stream of values that requires an environment, can fail with a typed error, and supports backpressure.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.fork
item.description:
Starts a flow in a new fiber without waiting for it to complete.

Parameters

  • flow: The flow to fork.

Returns

A flow that produces a Fiber handle.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.fromOption
item.description:
Lifts an option into a synchronous flow with the supplied error.

Examples

let opt = Some "value"
 let flow = Flow.fromOption "missing" opt

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.EnvironmentVariables.fromPairs
item.description: Creates a deterministic provider from a fixed set of name/value pairs.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.fromPredicate
item.description:
Builds a check from a predicate while preserving the successful value.

Parameters

  • predicate: A function of type 'value -> bool to test the value.
  • value: The value of type 'value to check.

Returns

A Check containing the value if the predicate succeeds.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.fromResult
item.description:
Lifts a Result into a synchronous flow.

Examples

let res = Ok "success"
 let flow = Flow.fromResult res

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.fromResult
item.description:
Lifts a standard Result into the Validation context.

Remarks

If the result is an error, it is wrapped in a root-level Diagnostics graph.

Parameters

  • result: The result to lift.

Returns

A Validation mirroring the result.

item.title: FsFlow.FlowStreamModule.fromSeq
item.description: Creates a stream from a sequence of values.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.Clock.fromValue
item.description: Creates a deterministic clock that always returns the supplied instant.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.Guid.fromValue
item.description: Creates a deterministic GUID generator that always returns the supplied value.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.Random.fromValue
item.description: Creates a deterministic random generator that always returns the supplied value.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.fromValueOption
item.description:
Lifts a value option into a synchronous flow with the supplied error.

Parameters

  • error: The error to return if the value option is ValueNone.
  • value: The value option to lift.

Returns

A Flow that succeeds with the option’s value or fails with the provided error.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.EnvironmentVariable.get
item.description: Reads a raw string environment variable from the environment.

item.title: FsFlow.RefModule.get
item.description: Reads the current value of the reference.

item.title: FsFlow.TRefModule.get
item.description: Reads the current value of the transactional reference within a transaction.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.EnvironmentVariable.getBool
item.description: Reads a boolean environment variable from the environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.EnvironmentVariable.getGuid
item.description: Reads a GUID environment variable from the environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.EnvironmentVariable.getInt
item.description: Reads an integer environment variable from the environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Http.HttpModule.getString
item.description: Sends a GET request using the HTTP environment and returns the response body.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.IClock
item.description: Provides synchronous access to the current UTC clock.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Console.IConsole
item.description: Provides synchronous access to standard console I/O.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.IEnvironmentVariables
item.description: Provides synchronous environment-variable lookup.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.FileSystem.IFileSystem
item.description: Provides synchronous access to file system operations.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.ignore
item.description: Maps the successful value of a synchronous flow to unit.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.ignore
item.description:
Maps a successful validation value to unit while preserving the diagnostics.

Parameters

  • validation: The source validation.

Returns

A validation that keeps the original diagnostics and discards the success value.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.IGuid
item.description: Provides synchronous GUID generation.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Http.IHttp
item.description: Provides asynchronous access to HTTP client operations.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.index
item.description:
Prefixes a validation with an indexed branch.

Parameters

  • index: The branch index.
  • validation: The validation to scope.

Returns

A validation whose diagnostics are prefixed with Index index.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.interrupt
item.description:
Signals a fiber to stop and waits for it to finish its cleanup.

Parameters

  • fiber: The fiber to interrupt.

Returns

A flow that completes with the fiber’s final outcome after interruption.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Process.IProcess
item.description: Provides asynchronous access to external process execution.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.IRandom
item.description: Provides synchronous random-number generation.

item.title: FsFlow.ScheduleModule.jittered
item.description: Adds random jitter to a schedule’s delay.

item.title: FsFlow.ScheduleModule.jittered
item.description: Adds random jitter to a schedule’s delay.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.join
item.description:
Waits for a fiber to complete and returns its final outcome.

Parameters

  • fiber: The fiber to join.

Returns

A flow that completes with the fiber’s outcome.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.key
item.description:
Prefixes a validation with a keyed branch.

Parameters

  • key: The branch key.
  • validation: The validation to scope.

Returns

A validation whose diagnostics are prefixed with Key key.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Console.ConsoleModule.live
item.description: Creates a live console backed by Console.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.Clock.live
item.description: Creates a live clock backed by UtcNow.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.EnvironmentVariables.live
item.description: Creates a live provider backed by the current process environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.Guid.live
item.description: Creates a live GUID generator backed by NewGuid.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.Random.live
item.description: Creates a live random-number generator backed by Random.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.FileSystem.FileSystemModule.live
item.description: Creates a live file system backed by File.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Http.HttpModule.live
item.description: Creates a live HTTP client backed by HttpClient.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Process.ProcessModule.live
item.description: Creates a live process runner backed by Process.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.localEnv
item.description: Transforms the environment before running the flow.

item.title: FsFlow.RefModule.make
item.description: Creates a new Ref with the initial value.

item.title: FsFlow.TRefModule.make
item.description: Creates a new TRef with the initial value.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.map
item.description:
Maps the successful value of a synchronous flow.

Remarks

If the source flow fails, the mapper is not executed, and the error is preserved. This allows for safe transformation of data within the flow.

Parameters

  • mapper: A function of type 'value -> 'next to transform the successful value.
  • flow: The source flow of type Flow to transform.

Returns

A new Flow with the transformed success value of type 'next.

item.title: FsFlow.FlowStreamModule.map
item.description: Maps the successful values of a stream.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.map
item.description:
Maps the successful value of a validation.

Parameters

  • mapper: A function of type 'value -> 'next.
  • validation: The source Validation.

Returns

A validation with the transformed success value.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.map2
item.description: Combines two flows with a mapping function.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.map2
item.description:
Combines two validations, accumulating errors if both fail.

Remarks

This is the core applicative operation. If both left and right fail, their diagnostics graphs are merged.

Parameters

  • mapper: A function of type 'left -> 'right -> 'value.
  • left: The first validation.
  • right: The second validation.

Returns

A validation with the combined result.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.map3
item.description: Combines three flows with a mapping function.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.map3
item.description:
Combines three validations, accumulating errors when any input fails.

Parameters

  • mapper: A function of type 'left -> 'middle -> 'right -> 'value.
  • left: The first validation.
  • middle: The second validation.
  • right: The third validation.

Returns

A validation with the combined result.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.mapError
item.description:
Maps the error value of a synchronous flow.

Remarks

Transforms the error type of the flow while leaving successful values untouched. Useful for mapping internal errors into public-facing domain errors.

Parameters

  • mapper: The function to transform the error value.
  • flow: The source flow.

Returns

A Flow with the transformed error type.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.mapError
item.description:
Maps the error type of a validation graph.

Parameters

  • mapper: A function of type 'error -> 'nextError.
  • validation: The source Validation.

Returns

A validation with transformed error values.

item.title: FsFlow.DiagnosticsModule.merge
item.description:
Recursively merges two diagnostics graphs, combining shared branches and local errors.

Remarks

This is the core operation for applicative validation. It ensures that errors from sibling fields are collected together into a single structured graph.

Parameters

  • left: The first graph of type Diagnostics.
  • right: The second graph of type Diagnostics.

Returns

A new Diagnostics containing the union of both inputs.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.merge
item.description:
Merges two validations into a validation of a tuple.

Parameters

  • left: The first validation.
  • right: The second validation.

Returns

A validation containing a tuple of the results.

item.title: FsFlow.MissingCapability
item.description: Describes a missing service-provider capability.

item.title: FsFlow.RefModule.modify
item.description: Updates the value of the reference using the supplied function and returns a derived value.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.name
item.description:
Prefixes a validation with a named branch.

Parameters

  • name: The branch name.
  • validation: The validation to scope.

Returns

A validation whose diagnostics are prefixed with Name name.

item.title: FsFlow.Needs
item.description:
Describes the capability contract for a single dependency.

Remarks

Named cap-set interfaces inherit this contract once and then expose the dependency through a member such as Clock or Logger. Workflow builders can accept any environment that implements Needs<'dep>, which lets larger runtimes satisfy smaller boundaries.

Examples

type IClock =
     abstract UtcNow : unit -> DateTimeOffset

 type ClockCaps =
     inherit Needs<IClock>
     abstract Clock : IClock

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.Guid.newGuid
item.description: Reads a GUID from the environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.Random.nextInt
item.description: Reads a random integer from the environment.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.not
item.description:
Returns success when the supplied check fails.

Remarks

This is a logical “not” operation for checks. Note that it discards the success value and returns Unit on success.

Parameters

  • check: The source Check to invert.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the input fails.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.notBlank
item.description:
Returns the string when it is not blank.

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-blank string.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.notEmpty
item.description:
Returns the sequence when it is not empty.

Parameters

  • coll: The sequence to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-empty sequence.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.notEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are not equal.

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values differ.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.notNull
item.description:
Returns the value when it is not null.

Parameters

  • value: The value to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-null value.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.Clock.now
item.description: Reads the current UTC timestamp from the environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.ok
item.description: Creates a successful synchronous flow.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.ok
item.description:
Creates a successful validation result.

Parameters

  • value: The success value of type 'value.

Returns

A successful Validation.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIf
item.description:
Returns success when the condition is true.

Parameters

  • cond: The boolean condition to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if cond is true.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfBlank
item.description:
Returns success when the string is blank.

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the string is null, empty, or whitespace.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfEmpty
item.description:
Returns success when the sequence is empty.

Parameters

  • coll: The sequence to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the sequence is empty.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns success when the string is null or empty.

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the string is null or empty.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are equal.

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values are equal.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfNone
item.description:
Returns success when the option is None.

Parameters

  • opt: The option to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the option is None.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfNonEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns the string when it is not null or empty.

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-empty string.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfNotBlank
item.description:
Returns the string when it is not blank.

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-blank string.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfNotEmpty
item.description:
Returns the sequence when it is not empty.

Parameters

  • coll: The sequence of type seq<'a> to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-empty sequence.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfNotEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are not equal.

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values differ.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfNotNull
item.description:
Returns the value when it is not null.

Parameters

  • value: The value of type 'a to check for null.

Returns

A Check containing the non-null value.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfNull
item.description:
Returns success when the value is null.

Parameters

  • value: The value to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the value is null.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfSome
item.description:
Returns the value when the option is Some.

Parameters

  • opt: The FSharpOption to check.

Returns

A Check containing the value if present.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfValueNone
item.description:
Returns success when the value option is ValueNone.

Parameters

  • opt: The value option to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the value option is ValueNone.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.okIfValueSome
item.description:
Returns the value when the value option is ValueSome.

Parameters

  • opt: The FSharpValueOption to check.

Returns

A Check containing the value if present.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.or
item.description:
Returns success when either check succeeds.

Remarks

This is a logical “or” operation. It short-circuits: if left succeeds, right is not evaluated.

Parameters

  • left: The first check.
  • right: The second check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if either input succeeds.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.orElse
item.description: Falls back to another flow when the source flow fails.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.orElse
item.description:
Falls back to another validation when the source validation fails.

Remarks

This is a left-biased choice operator. If the source succeeds, the fallback is not used. If the source fails, the fallback validation is returned as-is.

Parameters

  • fallback: The validation to use when the source fails.
  • validation: The source validation.

Returns

The source validation when it succeeds, otherwise the fallback validation.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.orElseFlow
item.description:
Turns a pure validation result into a synchronous flow with environment-provided failure.

Remarks

This helper bridges the gap between pure validation (which often uses Result or Check) and the Flow environment model. If the result is an error, the provided errorFlow is executed to produce the final application error.

Parameters

  • errorFlow: A flow that reads the environment to produce an error value.
  • result: The pure result to bridge.

Returns

A Flow that mirrors the success of the result or fails with the outcome of the error flow.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.orElseWith
item.description: Falls back to another flow when the source flow fails.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.orElseWith
item.description:
Computes a fallback validation from the source diagnostics when validation fails.

Remarks

This is the lazy counterpart to orElse and is useful when the alternate branch depends on the accumulated diagnostics.

Parameters

  • fallback: A function that turns the diagnostics into an alternate validation.
  • validation: The source validation.

Returns

The source validation when it succeeds, otherwise the computed fallback validation.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.orError
item.description:
Maps a unit error into the supplied application error value.

Remarks

This is the primary bridge from checks to domain-specific results.

Parameters

  • error: The domain error of type 'error to return on failure.
  • result: The source Check.

Returns

A Result with the provided error value.

item.title: FsFlow.CheckModule.orErrorWith
item.description:
Maps a unit error into an application error produced on demand.

Parameters

  • errorFn: A function of type unit -> 'error to produce the error.
  • result: The source Check.

Returns

A Result with the produced error value.

item.title: FsFlow.Path
item.description: A path through a validation graph, represented as a list of PathSegment.

item.title: FsFlow.PathSegment
item.description: Location markers used to describe where a diagnostic belongs in a validation graph.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Process.ProcessResult
item.description: Represents the outcome of an external process execution.

item.title: FsFlow.LayerModule.provideLayer
item.description: Provides a derived environment from a layer flow to a downstream flow.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.provideLayer
item.description: Provides a derived environment from a layer flow to a downstream flow.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.race
item.description:
Runs two flows concurrently and returns the result of the first one to complete.

Remarks

The “loser” flow is interrupted immediately.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.read
item.description:
Projects a value from the current environment.

Remarks

This is the primary way to access dependencies or configuration stored in the environment. The projection function is applied to the environment at execution time.

Parameters

  • projection: A function that extracts a value from the environment.

Returns

A Flow containing the projected value.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.FileSystem.FileSystemModule.readAllText
item.description: Reads all text from a file using the file system environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Console.ConsoleModule.readLine
item.description: Reads a line from the console environment.

item.title: FsFlow.ScheduleModule.recurs
item.description: Creates a schedule that recurs a fixed number of times.

item.title: FsFlow.ScheduleModule.recurs
item.description: Creates a schedule that recurs a fixed number of times.

item.title: FsFlow.Ref
item.description: Represents a handle to a mutable reference that can be updated atomically.

item.title: FsFlow.Builders.result
item.description:
The fail-fast result { } computation expression.

Remarks

Use this builder when the happy path should short-circuit on the first error and you want to keep the workflow in `Result` shape all the way through. It works well for parsing, validation, and other boundaries where failure is expected to stop the flow immediately instead of accumulating diagnostics. Use `Check.orError` when a pure check needs a domain error, and `Guard.MapError` when you need to remap an existing error before entering the CE.

Examples

 let parsedUser =
     result {
         let! age = parseAge input
         let! name = parseName input
         return { Age = age; Name = name }
     }

item.title: Result Builder
item.description:
The result { } builder provides a fail-fast computation expression for standard F# Result values.

Builder

  • FsFlow.Builders.result: The fail-fast result { } computation expression.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.run
item.description:
Executes a flow with the provided environment and the default cancellation token.

Examples

let flow = Flow.read (fun env -> $"Hello, {env}!")
 let result = Flow.run "World" flow
 // result = Promise that resolves to Success "Hello, World!" on Fable, or Success "Hello, World!" on .NET

item.title: FsFlow.FlowStreamModule.runForEach
item.description: Executes the stream and performs an action for each value.

item.title: FsFlow.CapabilityModule.runtime
item.description: Reads the current runtime from the environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Schedule
item.description: Represents a stateful schedule that can decide whether to continue and how long to delay.

item.title: FsFlow.Schedule
item.description: Represents a stateful schedule that can decide whether to continue and how long to delay.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.sequence
item.description: Transforms a sequence of flows into a flow of a sequence and stops at the first failure.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.sequence
item.description:
Transforms a sequence of validations into a validation of a list.

Parameters

  • validations: The input sequence.

Returns

A validation containing the list of values.

item.title: FsFlow.CapabilityModule.service
item.description: Reads a service from the environment using the provided projection.

item.title: FsFlow.CapabilityModule.serviceFromProvider
item.description: Reads a service from IServiceProvider and fails when it is not registered.

item.title: FsFlow.RefModule.set
item.description: Sets the value of the reference to the specified value.

item.title: FsFlow.TRefModule.set
item.description: Sets the value of the transactional reference within a transaction.

item.title: FsFlow.DiagnosticsModule.singleton
item.description:
Creates a diagnostics graph containing exactly one error at the root.

Parameters

  • error: The application error to store at the root.

Returns

A Diagnostics with a single error.

item.title: FsFlow.ScheduleModule.spaced
item.description: Creates a schedule that recurs with a fixed delay between attempts.

item.title: FsFlow.ScheduleModule.spaced
item.description: Creates a schedule that recurs with a fixed delay between attempts.

item.title: FsFlow.FlowScheduleExtensions.Flow.Repeat.Static
item.description: Repeats a successful flow according to the supplied schedule.

item.title: FsFlow.FlowScheduleExtensions.Flow.Retry.Static
item.description: Retries a failing flow according to the supplied schedule.

item.title: FsFlow.FlowScheduleExtensions.Flow.Repeat.Static
item.description: Repeats a successful flow according to the supplied schedule.

item.title: FsFlow.FlowScheduleExtensions.Flow.Retry.Static
item.description: Retries a failing flow according to the supplied schedule.

item.title: FsFlow.StmBuilders.stm
item.description: The stm { } computation expression for building atomic transactions.

item.title: FsFlow.STM
item.description: Represents a transactional operation that can be composed and executed atomically.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.succeed
item.description:
Alias for ok that reads well in some call sites.

Examples

let flow = Flow.succeed 42
 let result = Flow.run () flow
 // result = Success 42

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.succeed
item.description:
Alias for ok.

Parameters

  • value: The success value of type 'value.

Returns

A successful Validation.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.tap
item.description:
Runs a synchronous side effect on success and preserves the original value.

Remarks

Use this for logging, telemetry, or other “fire and forget” operations that should not alter the primary value path. If the binder flow fails, the entire flow fails with that error.

Parameters

  • binder: A function that produces a side-effect flow from the successful value.
  • flow: The source flow.

Returns

A Flow that preserves the original success value after the side effect.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.tapError
item.description:
Runs a synchronous side effect on failure and preserves the original error.

Remarks

Use this for error logging or cleanup actions that depend on the environment. If the binder side-effect flow itself fails, its error will overwrite the original error.

Parameters

  • binder: A function that produces a side-effect flow from the error value.
  • flow: The source flow.

Returns

A Flow that preserves the original error after the side effect.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.toResult
item.description:
Converts a Validation into a standard Result.

Parameters

  • validation: The validation to convert.

Returns

A result containing either the success value or the full diagnostics graph.

item.title: FsFlow.DiagnosticsModule.toString
item.description:
Renders a diagnostics graph in a YAML-like layout for display.

Remarks

This is intended for human-readable output. Empty sections are omitted, and children are shown directly under their branch labels at the same indentation level as errors. Errors render as YAML-style bullet items without an Errors: key. Use flatten when you need path-bearing diagnostics for reporting or assertions.

Parameters

  • graph: The diagnostics graph to render.

Returns

A formatted string representation of the graph.

item.title: FsFlow.Runtime.Telemetry.Activity.trace
item.description:
Wraps a flow in a new activity and automatically maps metadata traits from the environment to tags.

Parameters

  • name: The name of the activity.
  • flow: The flow to trace.

Returns

A flow that executes within the activity span.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.traverse
item.description: Transforms a sequence of values into a flow and stops at the first failure.

item.title: FsFlow.ValidationModule.traverseIndexed
item.description:
Maps a sequence into validations while prefixing each item with its index.

Remarks

This is the indexed version of sequence. It is useful for list and array validation because each item can keep its own Index branch without the caller manually wrapping every item.

Parameters

  • binder: A function of type int -> 'source -> Validation<'value, 'error>.
  • values: The input sequence.

Returns

A validation containing the list of values or accumulated diagnostics.

item.title: FsFlow.TRef
item.description: Represents a transactional reference that can be updated atomically within an STM transaction.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.EnvironmentVariable.tryGet
item.description: Reads a raw string environment variable without wrapping it in a result.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Core.EnvironmentVariables.tryGet
item.description: Reads a raw environment-variable value from the environment.

item.title: FsFlow.RefModule.update
item.description: Updates the value of the reference using the supplied function.

item.title: FsFlow.TRefModule.update
item.description: Updates the value of the transactional reference within a transaction using the supplied function.

item.title: FsFlow.Builders.validate
item.description:
The accumulating validate { } computation expression.

Remarks

Use this builder when you want to collect all validation failures instead of stopping at the first one. Use `and!` when sibling validations should accumulate into the same diagnostics graph. Plain `let!` and `do!` are sequential: if the left side fails, the later step is not evaluated. `Check<'value>` covers both value-preserving checks and gate checks. Use `Check.orError` to attach an application error, and `Guard.Of` / `Guard.MapError` when you want the same error-bound source shape to participate directly in validation. When nested API response fields need to keep their place in the diagnostics graph, use the scoped helpers `validate.key`, `validate.index`, and `validate.name` inside the computation expression. If you already have a `Validation` value, use `Validation.key`, `Validation.index`, or `Validation.name` to prefix it after the fact. It is intended for forms, configuration checks, and other input-heavy boundaries where the user benefits from seeing every problem at once.

Examples

 let validatedUser =
     validate {
         let! name = Check.notBlank input.Name
         let! age = Check.okIf (input.Age > 0) "Age must be positive"
         return { Name = name; Age = age }
     }

let validatedCustomer =
    validate.key "customer" {
        let! name =
            validate.name "Name" {
                return! input.Name |> Check.notBlank |> Check.orError "Name required"
            }

        return name
    }

item.title: validate { }
item.description:
The validate { } builder is used for accumulating sibling failures into a structured diagnostics graph.

Builder

  • FsFlow.Builders.validate: The accumulating validate { } computation expression.

item.title: FsFlow.Hosting.Startup.validateEnvironment
item.description: Validates that all required environment variables are present and valid using the process environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Validation
item.description:
An accumulating validation result that keeps the structured diagnostics graph visible.

Remarks

Unlike FSharpResult, this type is designed for applicative composition using and! in the validate { } builder, which merges errors instead of short-circuiting.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.value
item.description:
Alias for ok that reads well in some call sites.

Examples

let flow = Flow.value "constant"

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.FileSystem.FileSystemModule.writeAllText
item.description: Writes all text to a file using the file system environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Caps.Console.ConsoleModule.writeLine
item.description: Writes a line to the console environment.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.zip
item.description: Combines two flows into a tuple of their values.

item.title: FsFlow.Flow.zipPar
item.description:
Combines two flows into a tuple of their values, running them concurrently.

Remarks

If either flow fails, the other is interrupted immediately.
